title: Lindernia scapoidea
intro: Lindernia scapoidea, also known as Scapoidea or Water Purlene, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Linderniaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Lindernia scapoidea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Lindernia scapoidea is typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, coughs, and digestive problems.

The family Linderniaceae is a small family of plants that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Lindernia scapoidea is further classified as a member of the genus Lindernia, which consists of several other species of plants.
